content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč and integers KKK and MMM. For 1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N1 \le i \le j \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N, let's define S(i,j)S(i, j)S(i,j) as the number of ways to choose exactly KKK elements of the contiguous subsequence Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,AjA_i, A_{i+1}, \dots, A_jAi‚Äč,Ai+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Aj‚Äč in such a way that the median of these KKK elements is ‚Č•M\ge M‚Č•M.

Find the sum of S(i,j)S(i, j)S(i,j) over all i,ji, ji,j such that 1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N1 \le i \le j \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N. Since this sum may be large, calculate it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: all elements of AAA are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ ‚ąĎi=1N‚ąĎj=iNS(i,j)\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=i}^N S(i, j)‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ąĎj=iN‚ÄčS(i,j) modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
